name: Shrimp and Gruyere Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups shredded Gruyere or Swiss cheese (about 5 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare tomato sauce separately.
2. Combine cracker crumbs and 1/4 cup melted butter. Press into bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
3. Chop cooked, cleaned shrimp.
4. Saute peppers, onion, and garlic in 3 tablespoons melted butter for 4-5 minutes until tender. Add chopped shrimp and saute another minute. Drain well and set aside.
5. Beat cream cheese and mayonnaise at high speed until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Gradually add milk, beating on low speed just until blended.
7. Stir in shrimp mixture, shredded Gruyere or Swiss cheese, and 1 teaspoon pepper.
8. Pour the mixture into the pan.
9. Bake at 300 degrees for 1 hour and 20 minutes or until set.
10. Turn off the oven and open the door partially. Leave the cheesecake in the oven for an additional hour.
11. Cool on a rack. Cover and chill, or serve at room temperature with hot tomato sauce.
Sauce:
1. Saute chopped onion and garlic in hot oil until tender.
2. Add tomatoes (break up with hands or spoon) and the rest of the ingredients. Simmer about 20 minutes, uncovered, until the desired consistency is reached. Remove bay leaf.
3. Serve hot over slices of chilled or room temperature cheesecake.
